节点.功能描述: Upload and download
Error code 201 - The permissions check fails.
Error code 401 - The parameters check fails.
Error code 801 - Call unsupported api.
Error code 13400001 - Invalid file or file system error.
Error code 13400002 - File path not supported or invalid.
Error code 13400003 - Task service ability error.
Error code 13499999 - Others error.
Code 0x00000001 - Bit flag indicating download is allowed when using the cellular network.
Code 0x00010000 - Bit flag indicating download is allowed when using the WLAN.
Error code 0 - Indicates that the download cannot be resumed for network reasons.
Error code 1 - Indicates that no storage device, such as an SD card, is found.
Error code 2 - Indicates that files to be downloaded already exist, and that the download session cannot overwrite the existing files.
Error code 3 - Indicates that a file operation fails.
Error code 4 - Indicates that the HTTP transmission fails.
Error code 5 - Indicates insufficient storage space.
Error code 6 - Indicates an error caused by too many network redirections.
Error code 7 - Indicates an HTTP code that cannot be identified.
Error code 8 - Indicates an undefined error.
Error code 9 - Indicates network offline.
Error code 10 - Indicates network type configuration error.
Paused code 0 - Indicates that the download is paused and waiting for a WLAN connection, because the file size exceeds the maximum allowed for a session using the cellular network.
Paused code 1 - Indicates that the download is paused due to a network problem, for example, network disconnection.
Paused code 2 - Indicates that a network error occurs, and the download session will be retried.
Paused code 3 - Indicates that the download is paused due to the user.
Paused code 4 - Indicates that the download is paused for some reasons.
Session status code 0 - Indicates that the download session is completed.
Session status code 1 - Indicates that the download session is in progress.
Session status code 2 - Indicates that the download session is being scheduled.
Session status code 3 - Indicates that the download session has been paused.
Session status code 4 - Indicates that the download session has failed and will not be retried.
Starts a download task.

节点.功能描述: File data structure. Defines the file list in UploadConfig.
When multipart is submitted, the file name in the request header.
When multipart is submitted, the name of the form item. The default is file.
The local storage path of the file (please refer to the storage directory definition for path usage).
The content type of the file is obtained by default according to the suffix of the file name or path.
RequestData data structure. Defines the form data in UploadConfig.
Represents the name of the form element.
Represents the value of the form element.
UploadConfig data structure. Describes the configuration of an upload task.
Resource address. Verification rule: Starting with http (s)://and with a length not exceeding 8192 characters.
Adds an HTTP or HTTPS header to be included with the upload request.
Request method: POST, PUT. The default POST.
The index of paths for a task. Usually used for a continuous job. The default is 0.
The start point of a file. Usually used for a continuous job. It will start read at the point in upload. The default is 0.
The end point of a file. Usually used for a continuous job. It will end read at the point in upload. The default is -1 indicating the end of the data for upload.
A list of files to be uploaded. Please use multipart/form-data to submit.
The requested form data in the request body.
TaskState data structure. Implements a TaskState object.
Upload file path.
Return value of an upload task. The value 0 means that the task is successful, and other values means that the task fails.
Upload task information.
Upload task interface. Implements file uploads.

节点.功能描述: The request agent api. Supports "background" and "frontend" tasks as while. Though "background" and "frontend" here do not the same with process's concept. All tasks will be executed at request manager service and recorded. Background tasks is for concurrent transfer, such as caching videos for a later play. Frontend tasks is for instant transfer, such as submitting forms for a consumption bill. Background tasks use notification to tell user tasks' status information. Frontend tasks use callback to tell caller tasks' status information. Background has some automatically restore mechanism. Frontend tasks controlled by caller. Uses `multipart/form-data` in client request for upload. A `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=<filename>` response from server leads to download. More details, please see the architecture documents of the request subsystem. Only front-end mode is supported in cross-platform scenarios.
Indicates to show completion notification.
Indicates to show progress notification.
The action options.
Indicates download task.
Indicates upload task.
The mode options.
Indicates background task.
Indicates foreground task.
The network options.
Indicates no restriction on network type.
Indicates Wi-Fi only.
Indicates cellular only.
Broadcast events for the request.
Completion event for the task. The code in the commonEventData can only be "0x40"(COMPLETE) or "0x41"(FAILED), same as "State". The data in the commonEventData contains the id of the task.
The file information for a form item.

The path to save the uploaded file. Currently support: 1: relative path, like "./xxx/yyy/zzz.html", "xxx/yyy/zzz.html", under caller's cache folder. 2: internal protocol path, starting with "internal://", like "internal://cache/path/to/file.txt". 3: application storage path, only the base directory and its subdirectories are supported, like "/data/storage/el1/base/path/to/file.txt". 4: file protocol path with self bundle name, only the base directory and its subdirectories are supported, like "file://com.example.test/data/storage/el2/base/file.txt". 5: user file url, like "file://media/Photo/path/to/file.png".
The MIME type of the file. The default is obtained by the suffix of the filename.
The filename, the default is obtained by path.
The extras for the file information. This parameter is not included in HTTP requests.
Sets the content-type field of the part. The default is obtained by the suffix of the filename.
The form item information for a task.
The item's name.
The item's value.
Describes the custom information of the notification bar.
The title of the notification. If not specified, use default style instead. The maximum size of title is 1024 bytes.
The text of the notification. If not specified, use the file name of the task instead. The maximum size of text is 3072 bytes.

The visibility of the notification. The notification information will be determined by the binary bit of the given value. The default value is 1, which means only completion notifications are displayed. The value cannot be set to 0.
The intent to send when click the notification.
Options of the minimum speed of the task.

The minimum speed of the task, in bytes per second. If the speed of the task is lower than this value for a period of time, the task fails. If the value is set to 0, no minimum speed limit will be activated.

Duration of the speed which is allowed to be below the minimum speed, in seconds. If the speed of the task is lower than this value for a period of time, the task fails. If the value is set to 0, no minimum speed limit will be activated.
Options of the custom task timeout.

The connection timeout of the task, in seconds. Connection timeout is the maximum time required for a client and a server to establish a connection. If this value is not specified, use default value instead. The default value is 60 seconds. The minimum value allowed is 1 second.

Total timeout of the task, in seconds. Total timeout includes the time to establish a connection, send a request and receive a response. If this value is not specified, use default value instead. The default value is 604,800 seconds(1 week). The minimum value allowed is 1 second. The maximum value allowed is 604,800 seconds(1 week).

The configurations for a task. Provides the configuration information of an upload or download task. Using a flexible configuration for clear upload and download functions. If without emphasis, an option is for any task.
The task action, upload or download.
The Universal Resource Locator for a task. The maximum length is 8192 characters. Using raw `url` option, even url parameters in it.
The title for a task, give a meaningful title please. The maximum length is 256 characters. The default is upload or download, consistent with its action.
The details for a task. The maximum length is 1024 characters. The default is empty string.
Indicates task's mode. The default is BACKGROUND. For frontend task, it has callbacks. For background task, it has notifications and fallback. The cross-platform default is FOREGROUND.
The solution choice when path already exists during download. The default is false. Currently support: true, rewrite the existed file; false, go to fail.
The HTTP standard method for upload or download: GET/POST/PUT. Case insensitive. For upload, use PUT/POST, the default is PUT. For download, use GET/POST, the default is GET.
The HTTP headers. For upload request, the `Content-Type` is forced to `multipart/form-data`. For download request, the default `Content-Type` is `application/json`.

The arguments, it can be any text, uses json usually. For download, it can be raw string, the default is empty string. For upload, it can be form items, the default is a empty form. There must be one `FileSpec` item at least or will be a parameter error.

The path to save the downloaded file, the default is "./". Currently support: 1: relative path, like "./xxx/yyy/zzz.html", "xxx/yyy/zzz.html", under caller's cache folder. 2: internal protocol path, starting with "internal://", like "internal://cache/path/to/file.txt". 3: application storage path, only the base directory and its subdirectories are supported, like "/data/storage/el1/base/path/to/file.txt". 4: file protocol path with self bundle name, only the base directory and its subdirectories are supported, like "file://com.example.test/data/storage/el2/base/file.txt".
Network used for the task. The default value is ANY (Wi-Fi or cellular).
Allows work in metered network or not. The default is false.
Allows work in roaming network or not. The default is true.
Enable automatic retry or not for the background task. The frontend task is always fast-fail.
Allows redirect or not. The default is true.
The proxy url for the task. Only this format is supported: http://<domain or IP-address>:<port> Username and password are not supported.
The index of paths for a task. Usually used for a continuous job. The default is 0.
The start point of a file. Usually used for a continuous job. It will set the "Range" header in download. It will start read at the point in upload. The default is 0.

The end point of a file. Usually used for a continuous job. It will set The "Range" header in download. It will end read at the point in upload. The default is -1 indicating the end of the data for upload or download.
The policy of the progress notification for background task. If false: only completed or failed notification, the default. If true, emits every progress, completed or failed notifications.
Breaks when fail to fetch filesize before upload/download or not. Uses filesize for a precise gauge. The default is false, set size as -1 indicating the case.

For in-application layer isolation. If given: the minimum is 8 bytes. the maximum is 2048 bytes. Creates a task with token, then must provide it during normal query. So saves the token carefully, it can not be retrieved by query. Or leave it empty.
The priority of this task. Front-end tasks have higher priority than back-end tasks. In tasks of the same mode, the smaller the number, the higher the priority. The default is 0.
The extras for the configuration. This parameter is left empty by default.
Use a single request to upload multiple files. If true, use the form format to merge multiple files into one request. If false, use independent requests to send each file. The default is false.
Customizes the notification of the backend task.
Customizes the minimum speed of the task.
Customizes the timeout of the task.
Indicate the current state of the task.
Indicates a task created by `new Task(Config)`.
Indicates a task lack of resources or conditions to run or retry.
Indicates a task in processing now.
Indicates a task failed once at least and in processing again now.
Indicates a paused task which tends to be resumed for continuous work.
Indicates a stopped task which must be started again.
Indicates a completed task which finish its data transfer.
Indicates a failed task which interrupted by some error.
Indicates a removed task which can not be processed again.

The progress data structure. Upload allows multiple files per upload task. Only one file in a download task. So using a unified data structure for progress. Generally: 1: sum(sizes) is total files size of the task. 2: float(processed)/sizes[counter] is the progress for the current processing file. 3: float(sum(sizes[:index])+processed)/sum(sizes) is the summary progress for a task. If fetch file size in failure, the size of the file in sizes will be set as -1.
The current state of the task.
The current processing file index in a task.
The processed data size for the current file in a task.
The sizes of files in a task, in bytes.

The extras for an interaction. Such as headers and body of response from server. But when the Content-Disposition header responded, the body will be into the uri of its attachment only, the body here is empty. {"headers": {"key": v}, "body": "contents"}. The "body" field is not supported in cross-platform scenarios.
Indicates the reason for the failure.
Indicates others failure.
Indicates network disconnection.
Indicates task timeout.
Indicates protocol error, such as 5xx response from server.
Indicates parameter error, such as url format error.
Indicates filesystem io error, such as open/seek/read/write/close.
Indicates DNS resolution error.
Indicates TCP connection error.
Indicates SSL connection error, such as a certificate error or certificate verification failure.
Indicates redirect error.
Indicates the speed of the task is too slow.
The filter data structure. Used for search, given fields works as **LOGICAL AND**. Invalid value may cause a parameter error.
Specify the end Unix timestamp. The default is the moment of calling.
Specify the start Unix timestamp. The default is "`before` - 24 hours".
Specify the state of tasks. The default is any state.
Specify the action of tasks, "upload" or "download", case insensitive. The default is upload and download.
Specify task's mode. The default is FOREGROUND and BACKGROUND.
The task information data structure for query results. Provides common query and advanced query, visible range of fields is different.
The path to save the downloaded file.
The url of a task. For `${ show }` and `${ touch }`.
The arguments. For `${ show }` and `${ touch }`.
The task id.
The task title.
The task details.
The task action.
Specify task mode. The default is frontend.
The priority of this task. Front-end tasks have higher priority than back-end tasks. In tasks of the same mode, the smaller the number, the higher the priority. The default is 0.
The MIME type in the configuration of the task.
An instance of `Progress` for a task.
The progress notification policy of a background task.
The creating date and time of a task in Unix timestamp. It is generated by system of current device.
The modified date and time of a task in Unix timestamp. It is generated by system of current device.
The retry switch of a task. Just for background, frontend always disabled.
The tried times of a task.
The faults case of a task.
The reason of a waiting/failed/stopped/paused task.
The extras of a task. For frontend, nothing now.
The HTTP response. Describes the data structure of the task response header.
The version of the HTTP response.
The status code of the HTTP response.
The reason of the HTTP response.
The headers of the HTTP response.
Reason for task waiting.
Indicates the task is waiting for running queue to be free.
Indicates the task is waiting for network to recover.
Indicates the task is waiting for app to return to the foreground.
Indicates the task is waiting for user to become activated.

The task entry. New task' status is "initialized" and enqueue. Can `start` a initialized task. Can `pause` a waiting/running/retrying background task. Can `resume` a paused background task. Can `stop` a running/waiting/retrying task and dequeue it.
The task id, unique on system. Generated automatically by system.
The configurations for the task.
